items.prompt: A combination solver has candidates indexed 0 through n - 1 and current start index s. Which indices should be considered as next choices?

options.text: s through n - 1.
0 through n - 1 at every level.
Only s + 1.
0 through s.

feedback.reason: Combination search enumerates each remaining candidate index at or after the current start boundary.
feedback.details: Passing i + 1 to the child prevents reusing the chosen index and prevents generating the same set in different orders.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.zero_to_n_minus_one: Restarting at zero permits reordered duplicates and index reuse.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.s_plus_one_only: The branch must consider every legal remaining candidate, not one forced index.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.zero_to_s: Those indices are already outside the remaining candidate suffix.

items.prompt: A permutation solver uses a used-by-index array. Which positions are legal next choices?

options.text: Every index whose used flag is false.
Only indices greater than the previously chosen index.
Only positions whose value equals the previous choice.
Only the first unused index.

feedback.reason: Permutation generation must branch over every currently unused input position.
feedback.details: Unlike combinations, the legal candidate range is not restricted to a monotonic suffix.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_after_last_index: That would generate combinations rather than all permutations.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_same_value: Permutation legality is based on unused positions, not matching values.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_first_unused: That would follow one path and omit other permutations.

items.prompt: A grid path may move orthogonally without leaving the board or reusing a path cell. Which operation defines the next-choice set?

options.text: Generate the four neighboring cells and keep only those satisfying bounds, match, and visited constraints.
Choose any unvisited cell anywhere in the grid.
Choose only the current cell again.
Choose only diagonal neighbors.

feedback.reason: Grid candidate generation starts from the movement relation and then applies branch legality constraints.
feedback.details: The choice set is local to the current cell and current path state.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.scan_all_cells: A path move is constrained by adjacency.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.stay_only: The no-reuse path contract forbids this.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.diagonal_only: The movement contract specifies orthogonal neighbors.

items.prompt: A partition solver is at start index s in a string of length n. Which exclusive end boundaries represent non-empty next segments?

options.text: s + 1 through n, inclusive.
s through n - 1.
0 through n at every level.
Only n.

feedback.reason: A non-empty segment beginning at s has an exclusive end strictly greater than s and at most n.
feedback.details: Every such boundary is a potential choice before applying the segment-validity predicate.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.s_to_n_minus_one: End = s would create an empty segment, and end = n must remain available.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.zero_to_n: Boundaries before s overlap already processed input.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_n: That tries only the entire remaining suffix and omits shorter legal segments.

items.prompt: When should a candidate that already violates a mandatory partial constraint be recursed into?

options.text: Never; it is not a legal next choice.
Always, because legality may be checked only at full depth.
Only when it is the final candidate in the loop.
Only when the candidate value is large.

feedback.reason: Candidate enumeration should expose only transitions that preserve the partial-state invariant.
feedback.details: Rejecting an illegal choice before mutation and recursion prevents invalid child states from entering the search tree.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always_then_fail_later: Known partial violations should not be expanded.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_if_last: Loop position does not repair invalidity.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_if_large: Magnitude is irrelevant unless the contract says so.

items.prompt: For a collect-all solver, can changing the order of legal candidate enumeration change the set of valid results?

options.text: It should not change the valid result set, though result order and traversal cost may change.
Yes, candidate order always changes which solutions are valid.
It changes the set only by removing the need for state restoration.
Any reordered loop necessarily creates duplicate results.

feedback.reason: An exhaustive legal search should be complete regardless of candidate ordering.
feedback.details: Ordering can affect short-circuit find-one results, performance, and the order of collected outputs, but not the mathematical solution set.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always_changes_set: Validity is defined by the contract, not loop order.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.removes_need_for_undo: Undo requirements depend on mutation, not candidate order.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.forces_duplicates: Duplicates depend on state and duplicate-control rules.

items.prompt: A nonterminal state has no legal next choices and does not satisfy the completion contract. What does it represent?

options.text: A dead end that returns to its parent.
A completed valid solution.
A reason to restart the entire search from the root.
A reason to append an empty result regardless of the current path.

feedback.reason: Candidate exhaustion and successful completion are separate conditions.
feedback.details: A dead-end branch contributes no result but does not invalidate sibling choices.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.valid_result: No-choice termination is not success unless completion is satisfied.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.record_empty: The result must satisfy the stated contract.

items.prompt: A solver for k-combinations has chosen c values and has only r candidate positions left. When can candidate enumeration stop immediately?

options.text: When r < k - c.
Whenever r equals the original input length.
As soon as at least one value is chosen.
Whenever any candidate remains.

feedback.reason: If fewer candidates remain than required slots, no child sequence can complete the combination.
feedback.details: This is a sound capacity bound that can shorten or eliminate the candidate loop.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.r_equals_n: That often describes the root, not impossibility.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.r_greater_zero: Remaining candidates are the reason to continue.

items.prompt: Why should candidate enumeration be based on indices rather than only values when equal input values may occupy distinct positions?

options.text: The result contract may distinguish using one occurrence from using another, even when values are equal.
Equal values cannot be compared.
Using indices automatically sorts the input.
Index-based choices never require undo.

feedback.reason: Candidate identity follows the problem contract.
feedback.details: Duplicate-control rules may later collapse value-equivalent branches, but the base candidate model must know which occurrences exist.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.indices_sort_input: Indices preserve positions but do not sort values.

items.prompt: Which properties define legal next choices for combination generation? Select all that apply.

options.text: The candidate index is at or after the current start boundary.
Enough later candidates remain to fill required slots when using capacity pruning.
The candidate preserves any active partial constraints.
Any unused index before start is always legal.

feedback.reason: Combination choices must respect ordering, feasibility, and problem-specific constraints.
feedback.details: The monotonic start boundary is what distinguishes combination enumeration from permutation enumeration.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.unused_anywhere: Indices before start would create reordered duplicate combinations.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.enough_remaining: This can exclude choices that cannot complete.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.candidate_constraint: Illegal partial states should not be entered.

items.prompt: Which properties define legal next choices for a permutation of distinct indexed values? Select all that apply.

options.text: The candidate index is unused in the current path.
The candidate preserves active assignment constraints.
Every unused position is considered rather than only positions after the previous index.
The value must be greater than the previous value.

feedback.reason: Permutation candidate enumeration ranges over all unused positions that remain legal.
feedback.details: A start boundary would incorrectly suppress valid orderings.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_greater_previous: That would impose an unstated monotonic-value constraint.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.index_unused: A position may appear only once.

items.prompt: Which checks can make a neighboring grid cell a legal next choice? Select all that apply.

options.text: The row and column are inside the grid.
The cell is not already used by the current path.
The cell matches the next required target symbol or condition.
The cell has never appeared in any earlier starting-cell search.

feedback.reason: Grid choices are constrained by geometry, target progress, and path-local visited state.
feedback.details: Global exclusion would incorrectly couple independent branches and starting positions.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.globally_unseen: A cell may need to be reused by a different candidate path.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.matches_next_target: The move must advance the target contract.

items.prompt: Which candidate boundaries can be legal in non-empty string partitioning? Select all that apply.

options.text: The exclusive end is greater than start.
The exclusive end is at most the input length.
The substring [start, end) satisfies the segment predicate.
The end may be before start to represent backtracking.

feedback.reason: A partition choice is a valid forward segment covering the next portion of the unresolved suffix.
feedback.details: Backtracking restores the path after exploring the child; it does not create backward or empty segments.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.end_before_start: Undo occurs after recursion; it is not represented by a reversed segment.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.end_at_most_length: The segment must stay inside the input.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.segment_valid: Only valid segments should become child choices.

items.prompt: Which statements support complete candidate enumeration? Select all that apply.

options.text: Every legal next choice is eventually considered unless a find-one contract has already succeeded.
Known illegal choices are excluded before child recursion.
Mutable state is restored before considering the next sibling.
Only choices that look intuitively promising are explored.

feedback.reason: Completeness depends on exhaustive legal coverage and branch isolation.
feedback.details: Heuristic ordering is allowed, but heuristic omission is not sound pruning.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.choose_only_promising: An intuition-based filter can omit valid solutions.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.consider_every_legal_choice: Completeness requires covering all legal branches.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.restore_between_siblings: Sibling choices must share the same parent state.

items.prompt: Arrange the candidate-loop flow for one backtracking state.

elements.text: Check that the candidate preserves the partial-state invariant.
Apply the candidate to branch state.
Recurse, then restore state before the next candidate.

feedback.reason: Legality is established before mutation, and restoration completes one sibling branch.
feedback.details: This sequence prevents invalid child states and cross-branch contamination.

items.prompt: Arrange the combination branch for choosing candidate index i.

elements.text: Remove the appended candidate from the path.
Advance the parent loop to the next candidate index.

feedback.reason: The child receives a strictly later start boundary, and the parent path is restored before the next sibling.
feedback.details: This produces each indexed combination once.

items.prompt: Arrange the partition-choice flow for a candidate exclusive end boundary.

elements.text: Form or reference the segment [start, end).
Append the valid segment and recurse from end.
Remove the segment before trying another end.

feedback.reason: A segment becomes a branch choice only after validation.
feedback.details: The path is restored so the next boundary is evaluated from the same partition prefix.

items.prompt: A permutation generator for n distinct values considers every unused value at each depth and emits all permutations. Select the number of leaves and maximum recursion depth.
constraints: Each complete path uses all n values exactly once.
No pruning removes a valid permutation.

feedback.reason: The branching choices shrink from n to n - 1 and so on, producing n! complete paths.
feedback.details: Depth follows the number of positions filled, while output materialization adds further output-sensitive work.
